text: The surgeon mobilizes the sigmoid and descending colon up to the splenic flexure, then begins dissection at the lower sigmoid and rectosigmoid junction. The dissection follows embryological planes as the surgeon enters the mesorectum, revealing the retrorectal mass laterally.

The surgeon extends the dissection laterally to achieve wider margins around the retrorectal tumor. Despite initial plans for sacrectomy due to the mass's apparent fixation to the sacrum, the mass is found to be free from the sacrum, allowing for adequate posterior margins without sacrectomy.
The surgeon identifies and carefully preserves the ureter, continuing the dissection down to the pelvic floor, ensuring the anatomical structures are maintained during the abdominoperineal resection.
The surgeon completes the anterior and lateral dissections, connecting the lines of dissection to achieve clear margins posteriorly.
The surgeon performs an extracorporeal colorectal anastomosis and creates a covering ileostomy. The resected specimen is prepared and sent for biopsy.
